summary.intent: Optimize the `saveGitOidMapping` call in the indexer to avoid redundant DB reads on every index pass for every checkpoint.
summary.outcome: Pre-fetched existing mappings into a `Map<checkpointId, gitOid>` before the loop, then updated `saveGitOidMapping` to skip the DB write only when both the checkpoint ID and git OID match exactly, allowing re-mapping after a rebase.

repo: gossamer/src/indexer.ts contains `indexAllCheckpointsV2` and `indexCheckpointV2` â€” the main indexing loop.
The Prisma schema uses `gitOid` as `@id` on the git OID mapping table, meaning a rebase scenario requires upsert logic rather than a simple existence check.
Checkpoint-to-git-OID mappings can be manually changed (e.g., after a rebase), so the cache key must be the (checkpointId, gitOid) pair, not just checkpointId.

code.finding: `indexAllCheckpointsV2` now pre-fetches all existing checkpointâ†’gitOid mappings with a single `findMany` into a `Map<string, string>` before the loop.
`indexCheckpointV2` accepts an optional `mappedCheckpointIds` parameter (refactored to a Map) and delegates skip/upsert logic to `saveGitOidMapping`.
`saveGitOidMapping` resolves the git commit first, compares against the pre-fetched map, skips if the exact (checkpointId, gitOid) pair already exists, and upserts if the OID changed.

workflow: Build validation with `npm run build` was used after each edit to confirm TypeScript compiled cleanly.
Requirement clarification mid-task (rebase scenario) prompted a design revision from Set to Map â€” good practice to clarify cache invalidation semantics before implementing.

friction: Initial design used a `Set<checkpointId>` which was insufficient â€” the rebase scenario was not considered until the user explicitly raised it, requiring a second refactor pass.
open_items: The in-memory Map is updated after a successful mapping but only within a single index pass; a long-running process restarting between passes will re-fetch correctly, but concurrent indexer instances could still race.

The upsert path in `saveGitOidMapping` for changed OIDs should be verified against the Prisma schema's `@id` constraint on `gitOid` â€” if the old record must be deleted before inserting the new one, an upsert may not behave as expected.
